Navigation here

Bucket policy examples

This section presents examples of typical use cases for bucket policies. The policies use example-bucket as the resource value. To test these policies, replace the bucket name with your actual bucket name.

Granting permissions to multiple accounts

The following example policy grants the s3:PutObject and s3:PutObjectAcl permissions to multiple AWS accounts.

{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Sid": "AddCannedAcl",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Principal": {
                "AWS": [
                    "arn:aws:iam::111122223333:root",
                    "arn:aws:iam::444455556666:root"
                ]
            },
            "Action": [
                "s3:PutObject",
                "s3:PutObjectAcl"
            ],
            "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::example-bucket/*",
            "Condition": {
                "StringEquals": {
                    "s3:x-amz-acl": [
                        "public-read"
                    ]
                }
            }
        }
    ]
}

Granting read-only permission to an anonymous user

The following example policy grants the s3:GetObject permission to any public anonymous user. This is equivalent to a public read-only policy.

Warning

Use caution when granting anonymous access to your Amazon S3 bucket. When you grant anonymous access, anyone in the world can access your bucket. We highly recommend that you never grant any kind of anonymous write access to your S3 bucket.

{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Sid": "PublicRead",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Principal": "*",
            "Action": [
                "s3:GetObject",
                "s3:GetObjectVersion"
            ],
            "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::example-bucket/*"
        }
    ]
}

Service quotas for S3

Resource Default quota Adjustable
Buckets per account 100 Yes
Object size (max) 5 TB No
Parts per upload 10,000 No

Using the AWS CLI

You can also use the AWS CLI to manage bucket policies. The following command applies a policy:

$ aws s3api put-bucket-policy --bucket example-bucket --policy file://policy.json

Verifying the policy

After applying the policy, verify it with the following command:

$ aws s3api get-bucket-policy --bucket example-bucket

Related resources

  • Access policy language overview
  • Bucket policy examples in the Amazon S3 Developer Guide
  • IAM JSON policy reference
